58 - What to do When a Prospect Ghosts You… - YouTube.mp4
All right, today's training, I'm gonna show you what to say, what to do when a prospect actually ghosts you, right? You've had a great conversation, maybe you booked in another appointment. Maybe they're about to pay and all of a sudden they go missing in action. What do you say? What do you do? Oh my gosh. Come over here. I'm going to to show you what most salespeople do and how they lose the deal when they try to follow up, compared to what you actually do to get the prospect to come back to you. You're not going to want to miss this, I can assure you. So look at here, this is a keynote that... I'm about to do here. So look at this. This is how most people, this is how most salespeople follow.
They say these big words like, 'Hey, Sally,' just circling back with you. It was a pleasure talking to you the past several weeks. We'd scheduled an appointment with you again about a week ago, but something must've come up for you and we didn't hear back to reschedule. I'm praying that you're okay. Does the prospect really believe you're praying for them? Unlikely. We'd love to work with you here at XYZ Company. We take pride in the work we do with our clients and we know we can do the same level of work that would make you feel proud and help you in achieving. Your retirement goals, let's say, if you sold financial services. We've been able to help many different people, like XYZ, ABC, YYY, OOO, and many others, achieve their goals and are very proud of the work we do.
Remember, we have a AAA rating with the Better Business Bureau, and our founders started the company with a unique vision. To be able to help people like yours solve today's unique challenges with our XYZ solutions. We look forward to hearing back from you and showing you all the things we can do for you and your family. Sincerely, Gretchen. Colesworth. Don't be like Gretchen. How many of your prospects do you genuinely believe even read more than one sentence? Of this email. Ask yourself, when you go to salesperson and email them some six-paragraph email, how much of that are you actually reading? Unlikely. You're probably gonna read the first sentence. And then delete it. It's the same thing with your prospects. So come up back over to the vibe board.
I'm going to show you the right way to do this where they actually respond once it goes to you. Now, let's go over. What do you do now? Now, once again, I just want to specify this is not for a, this does not work. What I'm about to show you. I'm not talking about a prospect. You've not talked to that. Maybe you had a one-minute conversation.
And they said, 'Call me back later.' That's not what I'm talking about. I'm talking about, you've already had a conversation. You've done a discovery call, maybe a meeting. They were moving forward. Maybe you're in B2B and you booked a second appointment, and they ghost you. Or maybe you're in B2C, business to consumer sales. They're ready to go. They have to go get their credit card. And then all of a sudden. They just go missing in action. Okay, what do you do to get them to re-engage? Now, prospect could be about to pay. They go MIA. Maybe you booked a second, third, fifth appointment. They go MIA. Okay. You've already left two, three voicemails, text, email. going to be the first voicemail or the first text message. It's typically about three to four messages in that I'm going to go over specifically how to get them engaged. All right? All right. Now, here's what you're first going to do right here. Now, typically, like I said, this is going to be your third or your fourth message. Okay. You've already left voicemails. You've already sent texts. You've already done emails. All right. Now, typically, if you sell business to consumer, you can send this out the next day after that last email or last voicemail you sent. If you sell more B2B, I wouldn't send this for probably two or three days after that last voicemail or last, you know, email or last text message you sent. It's a little bit different there.
Okay. All right. So here's what you're going to say. And I want you to write this word for word. Do not change the wording. Do not change the verbiage. I can assure you, if you do, you're not going to get the same result. Okay. Now. You're going to say, like, 'Hey, their name,' 'Hey, their name.' And then you go down a line. 'Hey, I did try to reach you a few times and leave a few messages, but we didn't hear back.' dot, dot, dot. Do three dots, five dots. I don't really care. It doesn't really matter. And then you're simply going to ask right below that. Now you're going to space out. Don't put this sentence right here where they... can't really see it where it blends in together like one paragraph.
You want to space it out like scroll down like one or two notches. And just simply say, where should we go from here? That's it. Hey Sally, I tried to reach you a few times and left a few messages last week, but we didn't hear back. Where should we go from here? And that's it. Or you can say, how do you want to proceed from here? So either, where should we go from here? Or how do you want to proceed from here? I personally like this. Better? Where should we go from here? It depends on the industry and a lot of it depends on you. But typically, we're getting like with our clients in your industry, we're getting like a 60 to 75% response to this.
Now, like I said, this is not the first email or the first text message you send out. This is after you've already probably left them a couple voicemails, emailed them a couple times, left a few text messages that they never responded to. Okay, this is like your third or... fourth attempt. I just want to specify to that. And remember, this is for only prospects you've had a discovery conversation with, not somebody you've never talked to. It's not going to work for that. OK, now, if you're like one of those like crazy salespeople that like have no sales ability because you haven't been trained by us yet or like. Like you were like one of those like hardcore, super pressure salespeople where it's like 'buy or die.' And at the very end, they're like, 'F you.' Like the prospect is like screaming at you, 'Is this going to work that well?' You're not going to get this percentage.
You got to learn back. You got to go and learn really how to sell the right way where the prospect qualifies for you and you learn how to bring, uh, what do I say? You learn how to build urgency in that process. You learn how to build internal urgency or internal tension, not external sales pressure, big difference. But internal. Internal tension in that prospect where they feel the pain of not changing their situation. And they see that this is going to keep being painful if they don't change. It's a whole different way to sell and you make way more sales. Can you imagine that? Okay. So you're going to learn how to sell the right way. Now, let's see if I did a voicemail. It's going to sound a little bit different.
Say voicemail. Hey, Sally, it's Jeremy Miner. I called you a few times last week. We left a few messages, but we didn't hear back. Where do you want to go from here? Now, I'm going to be available here for a bit today. If I don't answer, leave a message and I'll get back to you as soon as I have time later or tomorrow. Okay. So it sounds a little bit different, but this is strictly email right here. Okay. I don't want you to say anything else. Don't say. At the end of this, I'm looking forward to your response, or I'm really hoping to work with you, or I'm praying you're okay. Just don't say anything else but this word for word. I promise you, you don't want to mess that up, okay?
Now... What happens to, let's say, the 40? To, let's say, you know, what is it, the 30 to 40% that won't respond to this, okay? You want me to show you what to do next? Let's say they don't respond to that email or voicemail that you just sent or even text message, okay? You don't want to then just email them again or call them again the next day. Like I said, this is not your first or second attempt to reach them. We are talking about this right here— is probably your fifth message. Okay, let me change that a little bit. Okay. Uh yeah, so this is probably like one of your last attempts to reach them. Not your first attempt. You want to make sure you do this in the right sequence.
So after about four or five days after that last email or message I just showed you to send. Okay, once again, there's probably two or three messages before that, right? You got to make sure you do it in the right order. Then you can simply email back or call back and leave a message or even text back and say, 'We didn't hear back.' Did you give up on, and then you're going to repeat back. What they said they wanted, did you give up on, you could repeat back their problem, their main problem, so that you can, and you repeat back what they said they want. Did you give up on putting in the right operations team so you could scale to $12 million a month, or what actually happened? Okay. See what I'm doing now. Okay. Did you give up on solving the main problem so that you can, and you repeat back what they said they wanted? Now don't use generic terms. Like, did you give up on solving your problems? So you have a better situation. Those are too vague. It's too generalized. It means nothing to your prospect. You need to be very specific about the problem they said they have. And then, so they can, and they repeat back what they said they wanted. And then, at that, at the end of that, you're going to say, or what actually happened. That's all you're going to do.

Where should we go from here? They don't respond. Three or four days later, I emailed back, 'Hey, we didn't hear back.' Did you give up on losing the 87 pounds so that you can walk your daughter down the aisle?
Or what actually happened? Now, you don't say— so you can walk your daughter down the aisle. When she gets married, if he never brought that up, right? Because then that would be kind of like a douchebag question, right? But if that prospect says, 'Man, I got to lose this 87 pounds.' because I'm afraid that when I, you know, five years from now, I might have a heart attack and I can't walk my two daughters down the aisle, then you would plug that in. If they didn't say that. You wouldn't say that. It wouldn't make any sense. All you're doing is— you're plugging in, like, 'Hey, they wanted the problem was, they need to lose the weight so they can and you repeat back, very specifically, not vague or generalized, very specifically what they wanted to do.
Once they solved that problem. Did you give up on losing 87 pounds so you're able to walk your two daughters down the aisle when they get married? What actually happened. It's very hard for the prospect not to respond to that. Okay. Did you give up on? Okay. My good friend, Chris Voss, FBI negotiator, just had him over. Here about a month ago on my podcast, spoke on some stages with him. He'll talk to you a little bit about that. Did you give up on losing? Did you give up on it? It's a technique that triggers their emotion. About, no, I didn't give up on that. Now it's very hard for them to actually give up on what actually happened. They'll always email you back with something like, 'Oh, I got busy.
I'm so sorry. I'm ready to get started.' Or, 'Oh, I got so busy. Because my mom went into the nursing home and I couldn't get back to you.' Or, 'We decided to, now's not a good time for us.'
